name: @forgeax/hello-custom-importer
description: End-to-end acceptance app: a host registers a custom passthrough importer (reel-game-blob) folded into pack-index via pluginPack({ importers }) and resolved at runtime through loadByGuid + a host loader (feat-20260629-importer-self-declared-fold-contract / M5 / w15).

forgeax._comment: smokeInvocation = self-contained SSOT (mirrors hello-sprite / hello-cube). hello-custom-importer exercises the host self-declared fold contract end-to-end: the reel-game-blob importer is injected via pluginPack({ importers }) at vite build time, folded into a DDC .pack.json + pack-index row of kind 'reel-game-blob', then resolved at runtime through loadByGuid + a host-registered loader and used to drive the scene (AC-01 real load + real use; AC-07 no Importer.fold method; AC-09 in smoke fleet).

bundle-size.reason: vite app bundle (dist/assets) is downstream of @forgeax/engine-runtime + @forgeax/engine-app + @forgeax/engine-rhi-webgpu sizes already tracked at the package level; demo workspace declares no separate bundle-size baseline

fps.reason: fps no-regression covered by hello-cube / hello-room / hello-app gates that run the default zero-overhead render path; this app's contribution is the host-importer asset pipeline, not a per-frame perf surface

bench.reason: no perf-critical hot path in this demo; the host-importer fold is a build-time one-shot, gated by smoke (structural + real load/use) rather than vitest bench

spike-report.reason: not a spike app; this is the feat-20260629 M5 acceptance demo
